title: Browserless Session
description: Schema for the Browserless Session object. Sessions are long-lived browser instances created via the /session REST endpoint that can be reused across BrowserQL queries and reconnected to via Puppeteer or Playwright using the returned browserWSEndpoint / reconnect URL.

SessionCreate.description: Request body / launch parameters for creating a Browserless session.

ttl.description: Session time-to-live in milliseconds before automatic teardown.

stealth.description: When true, route the session through the stealth profile (hardened fingerprint, WebGL randomization, entropy injection).

blockAds.description: When true, load uBlock-Lite to block ads and trackers.

headless.description: When false, run the browser with a visible window (only meaningful in self-hosted deployments).

humanlike.description: When true, add human-like timing jitter to clicks, scrolls, and typing.

record.description: When true, capture a WebM screen recording of the session.

trackingId.description: Free-form identifier used to tag the session for billing / reporting allocation.

type.description: Use a built-in residential proxy or a customer-supplied external proxy.

country.description: Two-letter ISO country code for proxy egress geography.

city.description: Optional city name for finer-grained proxy egress.

sticky.description: When true, keep the same residential IP for the duration of the session.

profile.description: ID of a previously created profile to attach (cookies, localStorage, authenticated state).

browserWSEndpoint.description: WebSocket endpoint used by Puppeteer (puppeteer.connect) or Playwright (chromium.connectOverCDP) to attach to the running browser.

reconnectUrl.description: Short-lived reconnect URL returned from BQL `reconnect` mutations, of the form /reconnect/{trackingId}.

liveURL.description: Optional live-URL for hybrid automation; participants can view or drive the browser through this URL.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp the session will be auto-released.

trackingId.description: Customer-supplied identifier used for billing / reporting allocation.

stealth.description: Whether the session was launched in the stealth profile.

recordingUrl.description: When record=true was requested, the URL of the captured WebM (available after teardown).
